stories.title: A Giant Storm Grounds Our Planes to East Asia
stories.headline_kid: A powerful typhoon named Bavi has forced Singapore Airlines and Scoot to cancel more than 20 flights to Japan, Taiwan and China.
summary: A typhoon is like a huge, spinning storm the size of many countries, with winds strong enough to bend trees and rain that pours down in buckets. This one is called Bavi. It has been battering Japan and is now creeping closer to Taiwan and China.

Because the winds are so ferocious, it is not safe for planes to take off or land. Singapore Airlines and Scoot have cancelled more than 20 flights and changed many timings. They also sent out a travel advisory, which is a friendly warning telling passengers to check their flights before rushing to the airport.

Singapore itself is far from the storm, so we are safe and dry here. But Singapore is a busy travel hub, like a giant bus interchange for the skies, so storms in other countries can still upset our holiday plans. Families flying to East Asia may need to rebook their tickets or wait a few days.
July is typhoon season in that part of the world, a bit like our rainy months. Countries there watch the storm closely and warn people early, so that everyone can stay safe until the skies clear.

lenses.me: If your family planned a holiday to Japan, Taiwan or China soon, your trip might be delayed, so you may need to be patient and flexible.
lenses.family: Your parents may have to call the airline, rebook flights or change hotel bookings, and keep checking messages from the airline.
lenses.friends: A classmate travelling during the school holidays could come back with a story about a cancelled flight and an unexpected extra day at home.
lenses.nation: Singapore is a major air hub, so when storms hit East Asia, Changi Airport and our airlines must quickly help thousands of stranded travellers.
lenses.world: Countries in Asia share weather warnings and work together so that people everywhere can prepare before a dangerous storm arrives.

compo_words.meaning: very fierce, strong and frightening
compo_words.example: The ferocious winds tore the umbrella right out of my hands.

compo_words.meaning: to interrupt something and stop it from going smoothly
compo_words.example: The sudden storm disrupted our long-awaited family holiday.

compo_words.meaning: an official warning or piece of advice
compo_words.example: The airline issued an advisory telling travellers to check their flights.

compo_words.meaning: to move something to a later time
compo_words.example: We had to postpone our trip until the typhoon had passed.

compo_words.meaning: wild, rough and not calm
compo_words.example: The turbulent skies made it too dangerous for the plane to land.

compo_words.meaning: to get ready for something difficult
compo_words.example: The villagers braced themselves for the powerful storm.

compo_phrases.meaning: to get through a hard or difficult time
compo_phrases.example: The travellers stayed calm and weathered the storm together at the airport.

compo_phrases.meaning: a quiet, peaceful moment before trouble arrives
compo_phrases.example: The sunny morning was just the calm before the storm.

compo_phrases.meaning: it is wiser to be careful than to take a risk
compo_phrases.example: We left for the airport early because better safe than sorry.

questions: Have you ever had a trip or plan cancelled at the last minute? How did you feel, and what did you do?
Why do you think it is important to follow safety warnings, even when they spoil our fun?
If you were stuck at the airport for a whole day, how would you keep yourself cheerful?
phrases: In my opinion, the most sensible thing to do is...
This reminds me of a time when...
One good thing about waiting patiently is that...
If I were in that situation, I would probably...
I feel that safety should always come first because...

sources.name: The Star — At least 20 flights from Singapore cancelled as Typhoon Bavi hits Japan
Yahoo News Singapore — 20 flights from Singapore cancelled as Typhoon Bavi nears Taiwan and China
Travel And Tour World — Typhoon Bavi forces SIA and Scoot cancellations

stories.title: Our President Visits Our Closest Neighbour
stories.headline_kid: President Tharman Shanmugaratnam is in Malaysia on a special visit to make the friendship between the two countries even stronger.
summary: President Tharman is on a four-day state visit to Malaysia from 12 to 15 July, invited by the Malaysian King. A state visit is a very special, official trip, a bit like being invited to a friend's home for a grand dinner where everyone is on their best behaviour.

Malaysia is Singapore's closest neighbour. We are joined by a bridge called the Causeway, we buy some of our food and water from them, and many families have relatives on both sides. President Tharman said the two countries should let their hearts 'shake hands', meaning they should be warm, friendly and work together.

When leaders visit each other, they talk about how to trade more, travel more easily and help each other grow. Good neighbours make life better for ordinary people, like having a kind family living right next door.
Singapore is a tiny country, so getting along well with our neighbours is very important. Friendship built on mutual respect helps keep the whole region peaceful and prosperous.

lenses.me: The fruit, vegetables and even the water you use every day may come from Malaysia, our friendly neighbour just across the Causeway.
lenses.family: Your family may travel to Malaysia for holidays or to visit relatives, so smooth ties make those trips easier and cheaper.
lenses.friends: Some of your classmates may have family in Malaysia, so strong ties between the two countries touch their lives too.
lenses.nation: Good relations help Singapore trade, share resources and stay safe, which is vital for such a small country.
lenses.world: When neighbours settle differences peacefully and cooperate, they set a good example for the rest of the world.

compo_words.meaning: to work together to get something done
compo_words.example: The two prefects cooperated to keep the queue orderly.

compo_words.meaning: shared or felt by two or more people
compo_words.example: Their friendship was built on mutual trust and respect.

compo_words.meaning: to make something stronger
compo_words.example: Playing together every day strengthened our bond.

compo_words.meaning: located nearby or next to
compo_words.example: We invited pupils from the neighbouring school to our concert.

compo_words.meaning: a close working relationship between two sides
compo_words.example: The partnership between the two countries helped both to grow.

compo_words.meaning: a friendly and kind feeling towards others
compo_words.example: The gift was a sign of goodwill between the two families.

compo_phrases.example: The two leaders did not always see eye to eye, but they stayed friends.
The neighbours worked hand in hand to clean up the void deck.

compo_phrases.meaning: to create friendly ties between people
compo_phrases.example: Sharing a meal is a lovely way to build bridges.

questions: Who lives next door to you, and how does your family get along with your neighbours?
Why do you think it is important for countries, just like people, to be good friends?
Can you share a time when working together with someone helped you solve a problem?
phrases: I believe good neighbours are important because...
In my family, we get along with our neighbours by...
One way to show respect to others is to...
From my experience, working together means...
If two people disagree, I think the best thing to do is...

sources.name: Ministry of Foreign Affairs — State Visit by President Tharman to Malaysia, 12–15 July 2026
The Star — 'Let our hearts shake hands': President Tharman on Singapore–Malaysia relations

stories.title: New Ways to Welcome Workers From Overseas
stories.headline_kid: Singapore is starting new programmes, including a special orientation course, to help workers who come from other countries settle into their jobs here.
summary: Singapore is a small country with lots of jobs, so besides local workers, some skilled workers come from overseas to help our companies. To help them fit in, the government is rolling out new initiatives, including an orientation programme that teaches them about our customs and how we do things at work.

Orientation is like the first day at a new school, when a buddy shows you where the canteen is and how the rules work. These programmes help newcomers understand local ways, such as being punctual, respecting different races and religions, and getting along with everyone.
The aim is for local and foreign workers to work in harmony and treat one another fairly. The government also wants to make sure Singaporeans are treated fairly and given good chances at work.

When people from many backgrounds work well together, companies do better and Singapore stays a friendly, welcoming home. Being considerate to newcomers is something everyone can practise, even in the classroom.

lenses.me: One day you may work with people from many countries, so learning to welcome others is a skill worth building now.
lenses.family: A parent's workplace may include colleagues from overseas, and getting along well makes everyone's day smoother and happier.
lenses.friends: You may have a classmate who moved here from another country, and a warm welcome helps them feel they belong.
lenses.nation: Singapore needs both local and foreign talent to keep growing, so helping everyone work together keeps the country strong.
lenses.world: Many countries welcome workers from abroad, and treating newcomers kindly makes the world a more caring place.

compo_words.meaning: to join in and become part of a group
compo_words.example: The new pupil quickly integrated into our class and made many friends.

compo_words.meaning: to change so you fit in with a new situation
compo_words.example: It took me a week to adapt to my new school.

compo_words.meaning: made up of many different kinds
compo_words.example: Singapore is proud of its diverse mix of cultures.

compo_words.meaning: a state of peaceful agreement
compo_words.example: The pupils of different races lived together in harmony.

compo_words.meaning: to give something helpful to a group
compo_words.example: Everyone contributed ideas to make the project a success.

compo_words.meaning: kind and thoughtful towards others
compo_words.example: It was considerate of her to help the new boy find his classroom.

compo_phrases.meaning: to help someone feel comfortable and welcome
compo_phrases.example: We made the new girl feel at home by inviting her to sit with us.

compo_phrases.meaning: to help someone who needs it
compo_phrases.example: I lent a helping hand to the newcomer who was lost in the corridor.

compo_phrases.meaning: in the same difficult situation as others
compo_phrases.example: On the first day, all the new pupils were in the same boat.

questions: Have you ever been the 'new person' somewhere? How did others make you feel welcome?
How would you help a new classmate from another country settle into your school?
Why do you think it is good for people from different backgrounds to work and learn together?
phrases: When I was new to a place, I felt...
One kind thing I could do for a newcomer is...
I think welcoming others is important because...
In my class, we make everyone feel included by...
From what I have seen, working with different people helps us...

stories.title: Coming Out of the Shadows
stories.headline_kid: Some young people in Singapore who shut themselves away in their rooms for years are now bravely trying to rejoin the world, with a little help.
summary: There are young people, sometimes called 'hidden youth', who stay in their rooms for months or even years, hardly stepping out or talking to anyone. It is a bit like a snail that pulls right into its shell and does not want to come out.

This often happens when someone feels overwhelmed by stress, worried about school, or hurt by unkind words. They are not lazy or naughty; they simply feel too anxious to face the outside world. A recent documentary followed some of these youth and their families.

The good news is that with patient help from counsellors, kind friends and loving families, many are learning to reconnect, step by step. Taking that first brave step outside can be as scary and exciting as speaking on stage for the very first time.

This story reminds us that everyone feels down sometimes, and that asking for help is a sign of courage, not weakness. A friendly smile or a listening ear can mean the world to someone who feels alone.

lenses.me: If you ever feel worried, lonely or overwhelmed, it is perfectly okay to tell a trusted adult, and doing so is brave.
lenses.family: Families are learning that being patient and gentle, instead of scolding, helps a struggling young person the most.
lenses.friends: A quiet or absent classmate might be having a hard time, and a kind word from you could brighten their day.
lenses.nation: Singapore is paying more attention to mental health, setting up support so that no young person feels left behind.
lenses.world: Many countries face this challenge too, and sharing what works helps young people everywhere feel less alone.

compo_words.meaning: to pull away from other people or activities
compo_words.example: After the quarrel, he withdrew and sat quietly in a corner.

compo_words.meaning: alone and cut off from others
compo_words.example: The new boy felt isolated until someone invited him to play.

compo_words.meaning: very brave
compo_words.example: It was courageous of her to ask her teacher for help.

compo_words.meaning: feeling that something is too much to handle
compo_words.example: I felt overwhelmed by the pile of homework on my desk.

compo_words.meaning: help and encouragement given to someone
compo_words.example: With my family's support, I felt strong enough to try again.

compo_words.meaning: to join or make friends again after being apart
compo_words.example: He slowly began to reconnect with his old classmates.

compo_phrases.meaning: to become less shy and more friendly
compo_phrases.example: After joining the choir, my quiet cousin finally came out of her shell.

compo_phrases.meaning: slowly and carefully, one part at a time
compo_phrases.example: She overcame her fear of the pool step by step.

compo_phrases.meaning: someone who listens and comforts you
compo_phrases.example: A good friend is always a shoulder to cry on.

questions: What do you usually do to feel better when you are sad, worried or stressed?
How would you reach out to a classmate who seems lonely or is always by themselves?
Why do you think asking for help is a brave thing to do, rather than a weakness?
phrases: When I feel worried, I usually...
If a friend looked lonely, I would...
I think it is brave to ask for help because...
One person I can always talk to is...
A small act of kindness that can help someone is...

stories.title: Kallang Fenced Off for an Army Exercise
stories.headline_kid: Parts of Kallang, including areas near Kallang Wave Mall and the old Kallang Airport, will be closed off on 13 and 14 July for a military training exercise.
summary: On 13 and 14 July, our soldiers from the Singapore Armed Forces (SAF) will hold a training exercise in Kallang. Some spaces near Kallang Wave Mall and the old Kallang Airport will be cordoned off, which means barriers will be put up to block people from entering.

The public has been advised to stay clear of these areas during the two days. It is important to know that this is only practice, like a fire drill at school, and there is no real danger to people living or shopping nearby.

Soldiers train regularly so that they are always ready and vigilant, in case they ever need to keep Singapore safe. Practising in a real place, rather than only in camp, helps them learn how to protect our city.
Singapore is small, so being well-prepared is one of the ways we look after ourselves. If you pass by, remember to follow the signs and take the safe route around the barriers.

lenses.me: If you visit Kallang Wave Mall this week, you may see barriers, so follow the signs and take the safe path around them.
lenses.family: Your family might need to plan a different route or car park if you are heading to the Kallang area on those two days.
lenses.friends: You and your friends might spot soldiers and vehicles nearby, and now you can explain that it is only a practice exercise.
lenses.nation: Regular training keeps the SAF ready to protect Singapore, which matters a lot for such a small country.
lenses.world: Many countries train their armed forces to stay prepared, hoping to keep peace by always being ready.

compo_words.meaning: to block off an area with barriers
compo_words.example: The police cordoned off the road after the accident.

compo_words.meaning: something done to keep people safe from harm
compo_words.example: Wearing a helmet is a sensible precaution when cycling.

compo_words.meaning: to protect from attack or harm
compo_words.example: The soldiers train hard to defend our country.

compo_words.meaning: watchful and always ready for danger
compo_words.example: The guard stayed vigilant throughout the night.

compo_words.meaning: to limit or keep something within bounds
compo_words.example: The teacher restricted us to the classroom during the storm.

compo_words.meaning: practice done again and again to be ready
compo_words.example: We had a fire drill after recess today.

compo_phrases.meaning: to stay away from something for safety
compo_phrases.example: The signs told everyone to keep their distance from the barriers.

compo_phrases.meaning: doing something often makes you better at it
compo_phrases.example: The soldiers train regularly because practice makes perfect.

compo_phrases.meaning: being well looked after and protected
compo_phrases.example: With our soldiers on guard, the country is in safe hands.

questions: Have you ever taken part in a fire drill or safety practice at school? What did you learn from it?
Why do you think it is important for soldiers to practise, even when there is no real danger?
How do you feel knowing that people work hard to keep Singapore safe?
phrases: During our school drills, I learnt that...
I think practising is important because...
One way I stay safe when I see barriers is to...
It makes me feel grateful when...
